what_it_does: Defines a React canvas for ConceptV voice and tone guidance, including static voice rules, vocabulary anchors, tone contexts, AI-assisted copy rewriting, tone variant generation, per-rule audit generation, saved rewrite display, and clipboard copying.
observations: The file declares VOICE_RULES, VOCAB, and TONE_CONTEXTS as local static data used directly by the UI and prompts.
The Voice component manages paste/rewrite state, tone generation state, per-rule audit state, and saved rewrite interactions.
AI calls use window.claude.complete with prompts that request compact JSON responses, then parse JSON with a regex fallback.
The UI renders a two-column rewriter, a tone variants generator, a visible voice rules section with per-rule audit buttons, saved rewrites when provided, and vocabulary cards.
There is a hidden display:none block that renders the voice rules without audit controls.
Several UI dependencies are referenced as globals, including CanvasHeader, ViShape, RefinePop, window.claude, and window.dsaToast.
standouts: Voice rules are both instructional content and prompt material for the AI workflows.
The per-rule audit flow generates likely violations and fixes for a selected rule rather than scanning source files in this file.
Saved rewrites preserve original copy, rewritten copy, violations, and a local time label.
conventions: Uses global React via const { useState: useState_v } = React instead of module imports.
Attaches the component to window.Voice instead of exporting it as an ES module.
Uses inline style objects heavily with dsa CSS classes and CSS custom properties.
Uses optional callbacks and optional chaining for saved rewrite actions and toasts.
Uses sentence-case UI copy consistent with the file's own voice rules.
Uses compact JSON-only prompt contracts for AI responses with fallback extraction.
concerns: The JSON fallback parser uses a broad object regex, which can misparse responses containing extra braces or multiple JSON-like blocks.
The auditRule UI says it finds likely violations across the system, but the prompt asks the AI to imagine typical SaaS strings rather than inspect actual files.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML is used for rule examples, relying on the local constants remaining trusted.
The hidden duplicate rules section appears unused from this file alone.
Clipboard writes are not awaited and do not report clipboard failures.
notes: This file is the interactive voice-governance canvas: static rules and vocabulary plus AI helpers for rewriting, tone variants, and rule-specific example audits.
questions: Should per-rule audits be hypothetical examples by design, or should they inspect actual product strings elsewhere?
Is the hidden voice-rules block kept for a transition, export, or earlier layout path?
Are window.claude, CanvasHeader, ViShape, and RefinePop guaranteed by the runtime shell?
Should saved rewrite timestamps use local time only, or a stable serialized timestamp?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it contains voice rules, vocabulary, an AI rewriter, and tone variants, with additional saved rewrite and per-rule audit UI.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make ConceptV's voice rules usable inside the design system as an interactive working surface rather than only a static reference.
novelty: Combines a voice style guide with live rewriting, violation reporting, tone variant generation, and rule-specific synthetic audits in one canvas.
contribution: This file contributes a voice-and-tone substrate: explicit rules, owned vocabulary, AI prompt contracts for copy transformation, UI patterns for reviewing violations, and a saved rewrite workflow that a fused system could retain as copy-governance behavior.
summary_for_embedding: React canvas for ConceptV voice and tone that renders rules, vocabulary, AI copy rewriting, tone variant generation, rule audit examples, and saved rewrite management.
intention_for_embedding: Built to operationalize brand voice guidance by letting users rewrite copy, generate contextual variants, inspect rule violations, and reuse approved phrasing.

symbols.does: Renders the full voice and tone canvas and owns its interactive state.
symbols.description: The component displays the rewriter, tone variant generator, voice rules with audit controls, saved rewrite library, and vocabulary anchors. It coordinates local state for AI requests, selected tone context, generated results, audit findings, and saved rewrite actions passed in through props.

symbols.does: Rewrites pasted copy against the ConceptV voice rules using window.claude.complete.
symbols.description: This async function builds a prompt from VOICE_RULES and the user's pasted text, asks for compact JSON containing rewritten text and rule violations, parses the response, and updates rewrite result state. It resets prior results before running and shows a toast if the request fails.

symbols.does: Generates tone variants for the selected context and brief.
symbols.description: This async function finds the selected TONE_CONTEXTS entry, sends a prompt asking for three distinct on-brand variants, parses a JSON variants array, and stores up to five results. It manages loading state and reports generation failure through the toast helper.

symbols.does: Copies a text string to the clipboard and shows a copied toast.
symbols.description: This helper uses navigator.clipboard?.writeText without awaiting the result, then calls window.dsaToast with a short confirmation. It is reused for rewritten copy, tone variants, audit fixes, and saved rewrite lines.

symbols.does: Generates example violations and fixes for one selected voice rule.
symbols.description: This async function sends the selected rule title and description to window.claude.complete, asking for likely SaaS UI strings that violate the rule and corrected versions. Parsed findings are stored under the rule id in ruleAudits, with toast feedback for success, empty output, or failure.

symbols.does: Adds the current rewrite result to the saved rewrite library through the provided callback.
symbols.description: This function guards against missing rewritten text or original paste content, then creates an entry with an id, original text, rewritten text, violation data, and a local time label. It calls addSavedRewrite when available and shows a confirmation toast.
